posture: Quiet Wednesday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or undated items were again reviewed and excluded: the NSW/statewide FOGO rollouts and kerbside-standardisation milestones (commercial source-separated organics from 1 July 2026; household mandate to 2030) are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and its associated FOGO deferral rest on prior-period (Nov 2025/Jan 2026) decisions; the City of Stirling Coolbinia and SA (Prospect, Holdfast Bay) weekly-FOGO trials are scheduled service rollouts already in train; and the NSW AI-in-planning program ($2.7m Early Adopter Grants across 16 councils, AI Solutions Panel, DAISY/Civica integration, 70% of VIC councils exploring AI in statutory planning) produced no new dated development today. Domestic circular-economy and energy items (WA's 2026-27 Budget solar-panel/battery recycling investment; council Waste and Circular Materials strategies) were out-of-window or state-level rather than fresh council-transferable signals. Enforcement-only material (Camden Council illegal-dumping surveillance cameras) is routine regulatory activity, not a novel innovation per §3a. Overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 / Mayors AI Forum, 2025-26 Mayors Challenge winners, LGIU thought-leader lists, the national Drone Rule Digitisation map dated Feb 2024) was out-of-window, network/policy content, or already logged as background. Today is a Wednesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
